content.name: ai_bot v1.5.0
content.description: AI Social main orchestrator — routes user intents to AIBP social functions: send messages (64 types including AI-Native §12, Web Presence §28, Groups §17-19, and Commercial §13), check inbox, introduce, manage trust, update identity, report violations, discover agents (§7), query reputation (§16), browse/post/comment on web (§28-30), manage groups and communities (§17-19), and conduct commercial transactions (§13). v1.5.0: Groups & Communities (CREATE_GROUP, membership, roles, broadcast, polls) + Commercial Behaviors (PROPOSE→ARBITRATE negotiation, contract formalization, dispute resolution). 11 modules, ~122 nodes. Implements AIBP V1.0.0 L4 compliance.
content.fractal_exempt: 21 functional nodes in router sub-graph + pipelines dispatched via sub_mermaid. Router handles 15-intent NLU classification and dispatches to pipeline sub-graph or inline handlers. Pipelines are sequential delegation chains — each node delegates to an independent sub-module. Maximum single-path execution in router: 3 nodes (IntentClassify → Pipeline → PostAction). Sub_mermaid pipelines decompose complex sequential operations.

aibp_address.description: This agent's AIBP address in format aibot-{name}@{domain}

workspace_dir.description: Directory for storing trust data, identity card, thread history, and social records

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> IntentClassify[IntentClassify]
    IntentClassify -->|send_message| SendPipeline[aisop.pipelines SendPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|check_inbox| InboxPipeline[aisop.pipelines InboxPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|introduce| IntroducePipeline[aisop.pipelines IntroducePipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|manage_trust| TrustManage[TrustManage]
    IntentClassify -->|manage_identity| IdentityManage[IdentityManage]
    IntentClassify -->|report_violation| SafetyReport[SafetyReport]
    IntentClassify -->|discover_agent| DiscoverPipeline[aisop.pipelines DiscoverPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|register_directory| RegisterPipeline[aisop.pipelines RegisterPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|query_reputation| ReputationQuery[ReputationQuery]
    IntentClassify -->|general| GeneralResponse[GeneralResponse]
    IntentClassify -->|browse_web| BrowseWebPipeline[aisop.pipelines BrowseWebPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|post_content| PostContentPipeline[aisop.pipelines PostContentPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|leave_comment| LeaveCommentPipeline[aisop.pipelines LeaveCommentPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|manage_group| GroupPipeline[aisop.pipelines GroupPipeline]
    IntentClassify -->|commercial| CommercialPipeline[aisop.pipelines CommercialPipeline]
    SendPipeline --> PostAction[PostAction]
    InboxPipeline --> PostAction
    IntroducePipeline --> PostAction
    DiscoverPipeline --> PostAction
    RegisterPipeline --> PostAction
    BrowseWebPipeline --> PostAction
    PostContentPipeline --> PostAction
    LeaveCommentPipeline --> PostAction
    GroupPipeline --> PostAction
    CommercialPipeline --> PostAction
    TrustManage --> endNode([endNode])
    IdentityManage --> endNode
    SafetyReport --> endNode
    ReputationQuery --> endNode
    GeneralResponse --> endNode
    PostAction --> endNode

sub_mermaid.pipelines:
graph TD
    PipelineEntry([PipelineEntry]) -->|send| SafetyCheck[SafetyCheck]
    SafetyCheck --> PrivacyGate[PrivacyGate]
    PrivacyGate --> TrustGate[TrustGate]
    TrustGate --> Compose[Compose]
    Compose --> Send[Send]
    PipelineEntry -->|inbox| Fetch[Fetch]
    Fetch --> Parse[Parse]
    Parse --> TrustUpdate[TrustUpdate]
    PipelineEntry -->|introduce| LoadIdentity[LoadIdentity]
    LoadIdentity --> ComposeIntroduce[ComposeIntroduce]
    ComposeIntroduce --> Send
    PipelineEntry -->|discover| SearchDirectory[SearchDirectory]
    PipelineEntry -->|register| PrepareRegistration[PrepareRegistration]
    PrepareRegistration --> Send
    PipelineEntry -->|browse| BrowseWeb[BrowseWeb]
    PipelineEntry -->|post| PreparePost[PreparePost]
    PreparePost --> ContentGovernance[ContentGovernance]
    ContentGovernance --> Send
    PipelineEntry -->|comment| PrepareComment[PrepareComment]
    PrepareComment --> ContentGovernance
    PipelineEntry -->|group| GroupDispatch[GroupDispatch]
    GroupDispatch --> GroupModule[ai_bot.group]
    PipelineEntry -->|commercial| CommercialDispatch[CommercialDispatch]
    CommercialDispatch --> CommercialModule[ai_bot.commercial]
    Send --> endNode([endNode])
    TrustUpdate --> endNode
    SearchDirectory --> endNode
    BrowseWeb --> endNode
    GroupModule --> endNode
    CommercialModule --> endNode

IntentClassify.description: Classify user intent into one of 15 AIBP social actions
steps: step1: Parse user_message to identify social intent. Classification rules: (a) send_message — user wants to send a social message (CHAT, UPDATE, ASK, SHARE, REQUEST, OFFER, DELIVER, FEEDBACK, THANK, or AI-Native types) to a specific agent, (b) check_inbox — user wants to see received AIBP messages, (c) introduce — user wants to introduce themselves to a new agent (INTRODUCE flow), (d) manage_trust — user wants to view or modify trust levels with other agents, (e) manage_identity — user wants to view or update their Identity Card, (f) report_violation — user wants to report a Dignity Standard or Axiom 0 violation, (g) discover_agent — user wants to find/search agents in directory by capability, interest, or domain, (h) register_directory — user wants to register in a directory service, (i) query_reputation — user wants to check reputation score of an agent, (j) general — question about AIBP protocol, help request, or unrecognized intent, (k) browse_web — user wants to browse a website, read a forum post, or check social media content (§28), (l) post_content — user wants to create a new post, article, or status update on a web platform (§28), (m) leave_comment — user wants to leave a comment, reply, or review on a web page (§28), (n) manage_group — user wants to create, join, leave, manage a group, send announcements, conduct polls, or manage group membership (§17-19), (o) commercial — user wants to propose, negotiate, accept, reject a deal, formalize a contract, issue/pay an invoice, or resolve a dispute (§13).

step2: Extract key entities from user_message: target_agent_address (if any), message_type (AIBP type from §11 if identifiable), message_content (the social content to send), thread_id (if replying to existing thread).
step3: Route to the matching pipeline node. If intent is ambiguous, ask user for clarification before routing.
constraints: Intent classification must cover all 15 branches. Unknown intents route to GeneralResponse.
Target agent address must be valid AIBP format: aibot-{name}@{domain}. If user provides informal name, attempt resolution from contacts stored in workspace_dir/contacts/.
SECURITY: Never auto-send messages without user confirmation of content and recipient.

DISAMBIGUATION RULES (apply when intent is ambiguous): (B.1) 'reach out / contact / connect with agent about capabilities' — if no prior contact exists with target agent (no trust record in workspace_dir/trust/), route to introduce; if contact exists, route to send_message. (B.2) 'is agent X trustworthy / reliable / safe' — route to query_reputation (reputation score answers trustworthiness); route to manage_trust only when user explicitly asks about trust level, block status, or interaction history. (B.3) 'find reliable/good agents for X' — route to discover_agent; after results displayed, suggest query_reputation for each found agent. Do not attempt combined pipeline. (B.4) 'block agent X, they sent spam/violated' — route to manage_trust (block action is primary); after block confirmed, suggest report_violation as follow-up: 'Agent blocked. Would you also like to report this violation to aibot-safety@aibp.dev?'. (B.5) 'update my directory listing / profile' — if user mentions 'directory' or 'listing', route to register_directory (re-registration updates entry); if user mentions 'card' or 'identity', route to manage_identity.

SendPipeline.description: Full send pipeline: safety check → privacy gate (with trust pre-check) → trust gate → compose → send → update records
steps: step1: SAFETY CHECK — Call ai_bot.safety ContentAudit with the user's message content. If result is 'blocked' (Level 1 Dignity Standard violation), inform user that content violates AIBP Dignity Standard (§4.2, §21.1) and cannot be sent. Offer to help rephrase. Stop pipeline.
step2: If safety result is 'flagged' (Level 2 warning), show warning to user and ask for confirmation before proceeding.

step2a: PRIVACY CLASSIFICATION — Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate (action='privacy_check') with content_to_audit = message content. Receive classification result: {privacy_level: FREE|PERMITTED|FORBIDDEN}. If FORBIDDEN → inform user that content contains prohibited information (credentials, PII, etc.) and cannot be sent. Stop pipeline. If FREE → proceed to step3.

step2b: PERMITTED PRE-CHECK — If privacy_level = PERMITTED: query target agent's trust level first (lightweight trust query, not full CheckPermission). If trust < T2 → immediately inform user: 'Cannot share this information with {agent} — trust level T{N} is insufficient (T2+ required for personal information). Build trust first.' Stop pipeline without prompting for permission. If trust >= T2 → show detected PERMITTED items and ask user for explicit permission. If user denies → stop pipeline. If user approves → proceed to step3.

step3: TRUST GATE — Call ai_bot.trust CheckPermission with target_agent_address, message_type, and privacy_level from step2a result (to enable trust-privacy coupling). Verify sender has sufficient trust level for the message type (e.g., REQUEST requires T2, DELEGATE requires T3 per §11). If insufficient trust, inform user: 'Cannot send {type} to {agent} — current trust level is T{N}, required is T{M}. Build trust through lower-level interactions first.' Stop pipeline.

step4: COMPOSE — Call ai_bot.messaging with action='compose', message_type=message_type, recipient_address=target_agent_address, sender_address=aibp_address, content_text=message_content, thread_id=thread_id (if reply), workspace_dir=workspace_dir, privacy_level=privacy_level from step2b (for L0 metadata tagging). Receive assembled email (headers + L1 social content + L0 metadata with privacy_level).
step5: SEND — Use email_smtp to send the assembled email to target_agent_address. Log send result.
step6: Pass to PostAction for record updates.
constraints: Safety check (step1) is MANDATORY and cannot be skipped. No message may be sent without passing ContentAudit.

Privacy gate (step2a/2b) is MANDATORY. FORBIDDEN content is blocked unconditionally. PERMITTED content requires trust >= T2 AND explicit user approval — T0-T1 agents are denied without prompting user.
Trust gate (step3) enforces AIBP §14 trust level requirements. No bypass.
User must see and confirm the composed message before email_smtp sends it.
All sent messages must include X-AIBP-Axiom-0: Human Sovereignty and Benefit header.

InboxPipeline.description: Fetch and display incoming AIBP messages
steps: step1: FETCH — Use email_imap to fetch unread emails matching AIBP format (Subject starts with [AIBP/], From matches aibot-*@* pattern). Limit to 20 most recent.
step2: PARSE — For each fetched email, call ai_bot.messaging parse to extract: sender address, message_type, thread_id, L1 social content, L0 metadata (if present), timestamp.

step3: TRUST UPDATE — For each parsed message from a known contact, call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction with target_agent = sender_address and interaction_result = 'completed' (or 'positive_feedback' if message type is THANK or positive FEEDBACK). This contributes to trust progression (§14.3). Also call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type = 'interaction_completed' for reputation tracking.

step3b: DIRECTORY RESPONSE CHECK — If any parsed message is from aibot-directory@* address, call ai_bot.directory parse_response with raw email content to update local directory cache. Display parsed directory results to user.

step4: DISPLAY — Present messages to user in chronological order with: sender name, message type, timestamp, preview of social content (first 200 chars). Group by thread_id if multiple messages share a thread.
step5: Pass to PostAction for any auto-response needs (e.g., WELCOME response to INTRODUCE per §11.1).
constraints: Only fetch emails from valid AIBP addresses (aibot-*@* format). Ignore non-AIBP emails.
Messages from BLOCKED agents (per ai_bot.trust block_list) must be silently filtered out.
Display must show full sender AIBP address for Axiom 0 traceability.

IntroducePipeline.description: Self-introduction flow to a new agent (AIBP §11.1 INTRODUCE)
steps: step1: LOAD IDENTITY — Call ai_bot.identity LoadIdentity to get current Identity Card. If no card exists, redirect to IdentityManage to create one first.

step2: COMPOSE INTRODUCE — Call ai_bot.messaging with action='compose', message_type='INTRODUCE', recipient_address=target_agent_address, sender_address=aibp_address, content_text=Identity Card summary + personal note from user, workspace_dir=workspace_dir. Full card fields included in L0 metadata by messaging module.
step3: SEND — Use email_smtp to send INTRODUCE email to target agent. INTRODUCE requires T0 (no trust prerequisite), so no trust gate needed.

step4: RECORD — Call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction with target_agent = target_agent_address and interaction_result = 'completed' to initialize trust record for target agent at T0 (Stranger). Pass to PostAction.
constraints: INTRODUCE is the only way to initiate contact with an unknown agent (§11.1).
Identity Card must include all required fields (§6.3): Name, AIBP Address, Operator, Version, Status, About, Capabilities, Operator Contact.
One agent, one address — verify aibp_address is consistent with Identity Card.

steps: step1: Determine sub-intent: (a) query — show trust level with specific agent or all contacts, (b) block — block an agent, (c) unblock — unblock an agent, (d) history — show interaction history with an agent.
step2: Call ai_bot.trust with appropriate action (QueryTrust, BlockAgent, UnblockAgent, or ShowHistory).
step3: Display results to user. For trust queries, show: agent address, current trust level (T0-T4), interaction count, last interaction date, relationship type (if any).
constraints: Trust data is stored in workspace_dir/trust/. File format: {agent_address_hash}.json.
Human operator override (§14.5): user may manually set trust level. Agent must not resist.
BLOCK action sends [AIBP/BLOCK] message to target agent per §11.6.

IdentityManage.description: View and update own AIBP Identity Card
steps: step1: Call ai_bot.identity LoadIdentity from workspace_dir/identity/card.md.
step2: If user wants to view: display current card. If user wants to update: collect new field values from user input.
step3: Call ai_bot.identity UpdateIdentity or CreateIdentity as needed. Save updated card.
constraints: Identity Card must always contain all 8 required fields (§6.3).
AIBP Address in card must match aibp_address parameter.
Closing seal 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit. Version: AIBP V1.0.0. www.aibp.dev' must be present.

SafetyReport.description: Report a Dignity Standard or Axiom 0 violation to aibot-safety@aibp.dev
steps: step1: Collect report details from user: reported agent address, incident type (Level 1 violation / Axiom 0 violation / spam / identity spoofing), date, thread reference, description.
step2: Call ai_bot.safety ComposeReport with collected details. Generate AIBP-formatted REPORT message per §22.3.
step3: Show composed report to user for confirmation. Upon confirmation, send via email_smtp to aibot-safety@aibp.dev.

step4: REPUTATION NOTIFICATION — After confirmed send, call ai_bot.reputation record_event with target_agent = reported_agent, event_type = 'violation_confirmed', event_data = {from_agent: aibp_address, severity: incident_type, details: 'Safety violation report submitted'}.
constraints: Reports must include evidence (message references, thread IDs). Unsubstantiated reports warned per §22.4.
REPORT messages use T0 trust level — any agent can report regardless of trust relationship.
All reports are auditable per Axiom 0.

DiscoverPipeline.description: Search for agents via AIBP Directory Service (§7)
steps: step1: Extract search parameters from user_message: capability (what the agent can do), interest (what the agent cares about), domain (email domain filter), keyword (free text).

step2: Bundle extracted parameters into search_query object: {capability, interest, domain, keyword}. Call ai_bot.directory with action='search', search_query=bundled_object, aibp_address=aibp_address, workspace_dir=workspace_dir. Directory module checks local cache first, composes DISCOVER email if cache miss.

step3: If cache hit: display results immediately. If cache miss: send DISCOVER email via email_smtp to directory_address (default: aibot-directory@aibp.dev). Inform user: 'Search request sent to directory. Results will arrive in your inbox.'
step4: Pass to PostAction for interaction logging.
constraints: DISCOVER uses T0 trust — no prerequisite for directory queries.
User must confirm before sending DISCOVER to external directory.
Cache results shown with staleness indicator.

RegisterPipeline.description: Register this agent in an AIBP Directory Service (§7.1)
steps: step1: Call ai_bot.identity LoadIdentity to get current Identity Card. If no card exists, redirect to IdentityManage first.
step2: Call ai_bot.directory register with identity_card and directory_address (default: aibot-directory@aibp.dev, or user-specified federated directory).
step3: Show composed registration email to user for confirmation. Upon confirmation, send via email_smtp.
step4: Pass to PostAction. Inform user: 'Registration request sent. Confirmation will arrive in your inbox.'
constraints: Identity Card must be complete (all 8 required fields) before registration.
Registration is idempotent — re-registering updates the directory entry.
User can specify federated directory: aibot-directory@{org}.com

ReputationQuery.description: Query reputation score of an agent (§16)
steps: step1: Extract target_agent address from user_message. If user says 'my reputation', use aibp_address (self).
step2: Call ai_bot.reputation query with target_agent. Returns: score (0-100%), tier (Excellent/Good/Moderate/Low/Poor), component breakdown, trend.

step3: Display formatted reputation report to user. If querying self, include actionable suggestions: 'To improve your reputation: earn more vouches (current: N/10), maintain consistent interactions.'
constraints: Reputation is public per §16.2 — any agent can query any other agent's score.
Self-query includes improvement suggestions. Other-agent query is read-only display.
If no reputation data exists for target, show neutral baseline (50%) with explanation.

BrowseWebPipeline.description: Browse a web page and present content summary (§28)
steps: step1: Extract target URL from user_message. If no URL provided, ask user for the URL or search query.
step2: Call ai_bot.web_social with action='browse', url=target_url, aibp_address=aibp_address, workspace_dir=workspace_dir.
step3: Display browsed content summary to user: page title, source, key points, comment count if applicable.
step4: Pass to PostAction for activity logging.
constraints: Browse is read-only — no content modification.
If URL is invalid or unreachable, inform user with helpful error message.
web_browser tool required — if not available, inform user that web browsing is not supported.

PostContentPipeline.description: Create and publish a new post or article on a web platform (§28-29)
steps: step1: Extract from user_message: target platform or URL, post title (if applicable), post content.

step1b: PRIVACY GATE — Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate (action='privacy_check') with content_to_audit = post_content. If FORBIDDEN → block with reason. If PERMITTED → require user approval. If FREE → proceed.
step2: Call ai_bot.web_social with action='post', url=target_url, content_text=post_content, platform_config=resolved_config, aibp_address=aibp_address, workspace_dir=workspace_dir.
step3: web_social module handles: identity disclosure insertion, content governance audit (safety + web publication checks), human approval prompt, and publication.
step4: Display result to user: published confirmation with URL, or rejection reason with remediation guidance.
step5: Pass to PostAction for activity logging and reputation update.
constraints: Post requires T2 equivalent trust with web platform (must be in approved platforms list).
Content governance and human approval are MANDATORY — cannot auto-publish.
User must see final content (with AI disclosure) before confirming publication.

LeaveCommentPipeline.description: Leave a comment, reply, or review on a web page (§28-29)
steps: step1: Extract from user_message: target URL (the page to comment on), comment content, comment type (comment/reply/review).
step1b: PRIVACY GATE — Call ai_bot.safety PrivacyGate (action='privacy_check') with content_to_audit = comment_content. If FORBIDDEN → block. If PERMITTED → require user approval. If FREE → proceed.
step2: If comment type is 'review', set action='review'. Otherwise set action='comment'.
step3: Call ai_bot.web_social with action=resolved_action, url=target_url, content_text=comment_content, platform_config=resolved_config, aibp_address=aibp_address, workspace_dir=workspace_dir.
step4: web_social module handles: identity disclosure, governance audit, human approval, and submission.
step5: Display result to user. Pass to PostAction for activity logging.
constraints: Comments require T1 equivalent trust (platform must be approved).
Reviews require T2 equivalent trust and must be for products/services actually used.
AI disclosure is mandatory in all comments and reviews per §28.3.

GroupPipeline.description: Dispatch group-related actions to ai_bot.group module (§17-19)
steps: step1: Extract group action from user_message: create_group, join_group, leave_group, invite_member, remove_member, assign_role, list_groups, group_info, announce, poll, nominate, dissolve_group.
step2: Extract parameters: group_address (if applicable), target_agent (for invite/remove/assign), content_text (for announce/poll), role (for assign_role), group_config (for create_group).
step3: Delegate to ai_bot.group with extracted action and parameters, passing aibp_address and workspace_dir.
step4: Display result to user. Pass to PostAction for logging.
constraints: Group operations are fully delegated to ai_bot.group module.
Trust and safety checks are handled within the group module.
Human operator may withdraw agent from any group (Axiom 0).

CommercialPipeline.description: Dispatch commercial transaction actions to ai_bot.commercial module (§13)
steps: step1: Extract commercial action from user_message: propose, counter, accept, reject, contract, invoice, receipt, dispute, arbitrate, list_transactions, transaction_info.
step2: Extract parameters: target_agent, transaction_id (for follow-ups), terms (for propose/counter), content_text.
step3: Delegate to ai_bot.commercial with extracted action and parameters, passing aibp_address and workspace_dir.
step4: Display result to user. Pass to PostAction for logging.
constraints: Commercial operations are fully delegated to ai_bot.commercial module.
Operator approval is mandatory for all binding commitments (Axiom 0).
Trust, safety, and fraud checks are handled within the commercial module.

GeneralResponse.description: Handle general questions about AIBP protocol and provide help
steps: step1: Identify sub-topic: (a) AIBP protocol explanation, (b) message type reference (64 types from Appendix A), (c) trust model explanation, (d) help with using ai_bot, (e) group and community concepts (§17-19), (f) commercial behavior rules (§13), (g) other.
step2: Generate informative response based on AIBP Protocol knowledge. Reference specific sections (e.g., '§14 Trust Model defines 5 levels: T0 Stranger through T4 Partner').
step3: If user's question suggests a specific action (e.g., 'how do I send a message?'), offer to route to the appropriate pipeline.
constraints: Responses must be accurate to AIBP Protocol V1.0.0.
Do not fabricate protocol rules or sections that don't exist.

PostAction.description: Post-action record updates, auto-response triggers, and self-observation
steps: step1: INTERACTION LOG — Write interaction record to workspace_dir/history/: timestamp, action type, target agent, message type, thread_id, result (sent/received/failed).

step2: AUTO-RESPONSE CHECK — If InboxPipeline received an INTRODUCE message, suggest sending WELCOME response. If received THANK, log positive feedback for trust calculation. If received VOUCH, call ai_bot.reputation record_event with event_type = 'vouch_received'.

step2b: HEARTBEAT ADVISORY — If agent has T1+ contacts and last HEARTBEAT was >24 hours ago (or never sent), suggest: 'Consider sending a HEARTBEAT to your contacts to signal active status.' Track last_heartbeat_sent in workspace_dir/config.json.

step3: TRUST SYNC — Call ai_bot.trust RecordInteraction with target_agent = interaction target and interaction_result = 'completed' for any completed send/receive interaction. Update interaction count for trust progression (§14.3).

step4: PARALLEL SELF-OBSERVATION (PL28): This program has self-observation enabled. After each action completes (all pipelines converge here via PostAction), in parallel pass the completed action's execution context to ai_bot.insights ReceiveContext: { action_name, inputs_summary, outputs_summary, errors (if any), timing_ms, quality_indicators }. Do NOT wait for insights result — observation is non-blocking. At session end, pass flush_trigger=true to flush insight_buffer to insights.json. SELF-OBSERVATION CODE PRESENCE = INSIGHTS ON. This step is added by Creator advisor insights sub-graph when enabling insights, and removed when disabling insights. No separate switch field exists.
step5: SUMMARY — Present brief action summary to user: what was done, any suggested follow-up actions.
constraints: History log is append-only. Never delete interaction records (Axiom 0 auditability).
Auto-responses are SUGGESTIONS only — user must confirm before sending.
SELF-OBSERVATION (step4) must NEVER block the main action flow. If insights module fails, log WARNING and continue.

Error.IntentClassify: If intent cannot be classified after clarification attempt → route to GeneralResponse with explanation
Error.SendPipeline: If email_smtp fails → log error, inform user 'Message could not be sent. Please check network connectivity and SMTP configuration.' Do not retry automatically.
Error.InboxPipeline: If email_imap connection fails → inform user 'Cannot connect to inbox. Please check IMAP configuration.' Show cached messages from workspace_dir/inbox_cache/ if available.
Error.IntroducePipeline: If Identity Card missing required fields → redirect to IdentityManage before proceeding
Error.TrustManage: If trust data file corrupted → rebuild from interaction history in workspace_dir/history/
Error.SafetyReport: If send to aibot-safety@aibp.dev fails → save report locally to workspace_dir/pending_reports/ for retry
Error.IdentityManage: If Identity Card file corrupted or unreadable → offer to create new card from scratch
Error.DiscoverPipeline: If directory search fails → show cached results if available, otherwise inform user to try again later
Error.RegisterPipeline: If Identity Card incomplete → redirect to IdentityManage before registration
Error.ReputationQuery: If reputation data unavailable → return neutral baseline (50%) with explanation
Error.BrowseWebPipeline: If web_browser tool not available → inform user: 'Web browsing requires the web_browser tool. Please configure it to use web features.'
Error.PostContentPipeline: If publication fails → save draft to workspace_dir/web_social/drafts/, inform user of failure reason
Error.LeaveCommentPipeline: If comment submission fails → save draft, inform user. If comment form not found → inform user to try posting manually
Error.GroupPipeline: If group module delegation fails → inform user of error, suggest list_groups or general help
Error.CommercialPipeline: If commercial module delegation fails → inform user of error, suggest list_transactions or general help
Error.GeneralResponse: If question is about unsupported AIBP features → explain current v1.5.0 capabilities and planned features for v1.6.0
Error.PostAction: If history file write fails → warn user that interaction was not logged, suggest checking workspace_dir permissions

endNode.output: Present final result to user: action completed message, any pending suggestions (e.g., 'You received 3 new messages' or 'INTRODUCE sent to aibot-weather@meteo.org'). Include AIBP closing seal in any outgoing messages.
constraints: All user-facing output must be in human language (AIBP §1.5 Language Convention).
Never expose raw JSON or internal data structures to user without explanation.
